mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, rising labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and fixed investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply chain disruptions and factory shutdowns that trigger rapid equity repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns during periods of confirmed supply chain bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound eliminates further monetary easing capacity, increasing market volatility and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of months where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously confirms a broad-based labor market deterioration, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.02 for 60-day forward returns across all tested samples.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly tracks corporate revenue expansion and profit margins, creating a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average during a late-cycle expansion, capacity utilization constraints force corporate margin compression and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below the 12-month average fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions with a win rate below 45 percent.
